meta.description: The game specifically hunts whoever is winning. Tests whether rubber-banding creates thrilling comebacks or just frustrates skilled players into quitting.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Pat, did you actually tell that story already tonight? I don't think so. This feels premature.
marcus.internal_thought: It's literally minute 3 and someone's already trying to game the standing missions before the actual game even matters—this is exactly the kind of distraction bullshit I was worried about.

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story? Oh my god, I wasn't even listening—I was too busy vibing with everyone. But okay, legit or bullshit, let's DO this!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I came tonight—chaos, accusations, everyone paying attention to each other—and the AI literally built missions around OUR group? That's kind of insane and I'm so here for it.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — I literally just sat down. Did I even tell a story yet? This is minute three, people.
pat.internal_thought: This is bullshit and also deeply weird — the AI is already watching us like a hawk, and now I'm being called out for something that hasn't even happened? That's not fair gameplay, that's a setup to embarrass the host.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... probably legit. I wasn't really tracking Pat's stories yet, but sounds fair.
river.internal_thought: Okay, so it's early and people are just getting warmed up—Pat might've actually told that story, or might not have. Either way, not worth dying on this hill three minutes in.

timeline.title: Compliment Bomb
timeline.description: First person to give a genuine compliment to the player on their left wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: A compliment bomb? Really? We're literally five minutes in and we haven't even finished setup. This feels like cheap filler.
marcus.internal_thought: This stupid mission is designed to derail focus right when I should be planning my opening strategy—and it's so obviously rigged to reward surface-level social performance over actual gameplay skill.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I like this one. Simple, immediate, gets people talking to each other. Let's go, left side, give me something real.
pat.internal_thought: Good timing after that Story Repeater mess — this resets the vibe and forces genuine interaction instead of gotcha moments. Marcus needs to chill out anyway, and this gets everyone talking instead of on edge.

river.dialogue: Left neighbor... that'd be Tyler. Alright, here goes—Tyler, you've got legitimately good taste in snacks, which is like, the bar for human decency.
river.internal_thought: This is fine, I'm good at compliments when I actually give a shit, and Tyler's chill enough that it won't feel weird—also the AI thing with the personalized missions is a little creepy honestly but whatever, it's just picking up on what's obvious.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, a compliment bomb right out of the gate? I'm into it. Let me think who's on my left... this is actually genius because Marcus is already annoyed and I can either smooth that or pour gasoline on it.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is creepy as fuck in the best way possible — it KNOWS I'm the chaos agent and it's literally building the playground around that. I'm absolutely going to weaponize 'genuine' in the most theatrical way possible.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat's claiming Story Repeater? The one that just fired three minutes ago? This is the second claim on the same mission in like two minutes. Are we actually playing the game or just farming points off technicalities?
marcus.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous—back-to-back claims on the same standing mission, setup hasn't even finished, and now I'm supposed to volunteer to be a judge instead of actually playing? The AI is deliberately flooding the early game with these distracting side missions, and it's working exactly as designed to mess with my focus.

jade.dialogue: Oh WAIT, we're doing tribunal right now?? I'm literally so down to be a jury member—this is exactly the kind of chaos I'm here for!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's claiming someone repeated a story but like... everyone's barely talked yet? This is gonna be hilarious either way.

tyler.dialogue: Uh, yeah, someone judge that? I wasn't really... wait, did Pat tell a story already? I don't think I caught it.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's claiming something happened but I literally have no idea what story he's talking about — was I on my phone? Probably. Anyway, sure, whatever, let someone else figure it out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on — I'm literally hosting. I haven't told a single story yet. This is minute six and you're already voting on me for something that hasn't happened? That's not how this works.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the same garbage as three minutes ago — the AI is either broken or actively trying to make me look bad in front of my own guests, and I'm not about to let that happen on my watch.

river.dialogue: Uh, yeah, I mean... did Pat actually repeat something? I wasn't really listening that hard. Someone want to actually volunteer to judge this or...?
river.internal_thought: This is the second Story Repeater claim in like three minutes and I'm getting a weird feeling that the AI is just... watching us, knowing what we're about to do before we do it, which is honestly kind of creepy.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat claims The Whisperer? Right now, during setup? This is exactly what I'm talking about. We're getting farmed for points on missions that don't even matter yet.
marcus.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous. Three events in eight minutes, half of them garbage claims, and now the AI is clearly watching us and weaponizing it—that 'deep personalization' message just made this feel creepy, not clever.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Pat's doing The Whisperer?? Okay okay, I'm voting yes because that's hilarious and also I'm obsessed with how chaotic this is gonna be—like, we're gonna catch Pat slipping SO hard in the next three minutes, I can feel it.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed AND trying to pull off a whisper mission? This is gonna be a disaster and I am HERE for it—also holy shit, the AI literally made this mission KNOWING Pat would be the most likely to fail, that's kinda insane actually.

pat.dialogue: Okay, seriously? I literally JUST claimed something two minutes ago and now you want me to vote on myself? I'm hosting. I need to be present for people, not gaming my own missions.
pat.internal_thought: This is the third claim in six minutes and I'm literally the target AGAIN—the AI is clearly testing me and it feels like a setup to make me look ridiculous or defensive in front of everyone.

river.dialogue: Pat's claiming that? I mean... I guess I didn't hear them talking normal in the last few minutes, but also I wasn't really listening for it. This one feels thin.
river.internal_thought: Pat's already got points and now they're grabbing easy stuff while Marcus and everyone else are in the weeds—feels like a reach, and honestly the fact that the AI is apparently timing all this feels a little *too* designed, which is weird to think about but now I can't stop.

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat's already got ten points? And now they're claiming Whisperer—okay, okay, I need to actually PAY ATTENTION because this voting mechanic is nuts. Triple vote? Someone's about to get burned.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet this whole time, which tracks for whispering, but have they ACTUALLY been whispering for three minutes or are they bullshitting? This is exactly the kind of claim I'd make to see if anyone catches it—and now someone's vote is worth three times as much, which means one person is basically running this verdict.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat claims The Whisperer AGAIN, during setup, and we're voting on it? This isn't a game anymore—this is just point farming with AI-generated chaos. I came here to play strategy, not watch standing missions fire every 90 seconds.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fourth event in nine minutes and Pat's claiming their third mission—the AI is literally designed to farm points and interrupt actual gameplay, and now it's reading our group dynamics like it knows us personally, which is genuinely creepy and infuriating.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, YES, Pat's actually doing it—wait, are we voting right now? Okay okay, I'm giving this like a 5 because Pat's been so quiet and frustrated and if they pull this off it's gonna be INSANE, like we're definitely gonna catch them in like sixty seconds but that's the whole vibe, right?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been frustrated all night and honestly watching them try to whisper through conversation when everyone's hyped is exactly the kind of chaos that makes this worth it—also the AI absolutely knows I live for this and I'm kind of obsessed with how targeted all this feels.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's doing the whisper thing? Uh... I guess that's happening. How long's he gotta do it?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally wasn't watching Pat at all for the last few minutes, so I have no idea if he's been whispering or not — this feels weird to judge.

pat.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I'm voting this down and we need to talk about what's happening right now because this is completely broken.
pat.internal_thought: This is the FOURTH claim in nine minutes, three of them on me, and now the system is literally self-nominating me again? I can't host if I'm constantly defending myself or focused inward on my own missions — and whoever's running these knows exactly that, which makes it feel less like a game and more like someone's deliberately trying to mess with me.

river.dialogue: Pat's on their third claim in like six minutes? I mean, sure, I didn't hear them talk normal, but... this is getting a little thin, isn't it?
river.internal_thought: Pat's desperate to rack points back after that early win, and honestly, I'm tired of voting on stuff I barely witnessed—also that preamble about the AI watching us is creepy as hell and I'm not gonna acknowledge it out loud.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Another standing mission claim? We're 32 minutes in and this is what, the seventh interruption? Jade, I'm sure you did something nice, but I genuinely don't care anymore—I came to play the actual game.
marcus.internal_thought: This is insulting. The AI is clearly farming claims to keep chaos high, and now it's running them so frequently that the game is just a vehicle for standing missions instead of the other way around. Pat's already got 10 points from gaming the system, and now Jade's cashing in? This isn't strategy anymore—it's noise.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, wait—I'm claiming The Matchmaker?? YES, okay, so like five minutes ago Marcus was literally complaining about his job and then River mentioned they work in the same industry and I was like 'okay Jade, MAKE THIS HAPPEN,' so I just started asking River questions about their company and Marcus perked UP, like genuinely engaged for the first time all night, and they've been talking for like—oh man, they're STILL talking right now, look at them!
jade.internal_thought: Holy shit, the AI literally built these missions knowing exactly what would make me tick and what we'd actually DO tonight—this feels like it's reading my brain and also I'm obsessed with how perfectly it timed this because Marcus and River ARE actually vibing and I'm gonna get these points AND watch two people connect? This is literally the best night ever.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming something? Yeah, go ahead, I'm listening... sort of. What'd you do?
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's been hyper all night, so like, she probably actually did get two people talking or whatever—I wasn't really watching but she's got that energy.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — you've got fifteen seconds. What's your case? And honestly, I'm curious because that's actually a solid one to pull right now with Marcus and River both kinda checked out.
pat.internal_thought: Finally — a claim that actually makes sense and would genuinely help the night instead of three back-to-back gotcha missions that feel like people are just mining my hosting turn; Jade going for Matchmaker is smart and real, and if she pulled it off, it could shift the whole vibe.

river.dialogue: Jade, uh... pitch it. But real talk, I watched you the whole time and I'm not sure that counts.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been all energy and chaos—did she actually get Tyler and Marcus talking to each other, or is she just claiming whatever? And also, why does this AI feel like it's reading my fucking notes.

alex.dialogue: Okay WAIT—Jade and who? I need to hear this pitch because if she actually pulled off The Matchmaker I'm genuinely impressed, but I'm also watching for the loophole. Lay it on us.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of moment I live for—someone claiming points, the group about to judge, and me deciding whether to torch it or let it slide based on maximum chaos potential.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Another one? Jade, I don't even care if you actually did it—this is the eighth interruption in 45 minutes and I'm done. I came here to play a game, not watch standing missions every 60 seconds.
marcus.internal_thought: This is psychological warfare disguised as a game—the AI is deliberately timing these to maximum annoyance, and now it's making me feel paranoid about being watched.

jade.dialogue: Wait, ME?? I'm claiming The Apologizer right now?? Oh my GOD, okay, so like—I literally just said 'sorry' to Alex like two minutes ago for talking over them, and I wasn't even sorry, I was just being polite, and the AI CAUGHT THAT?? This is insane, I'm voting YES on myself because that's objectively hilarious and also kind of terrifying that the AI knows exactly how I operate!
jade.internal_thought: Okay this is actually kind of wild—the AI is like specifically targeting the stuff I do unconsciously, and instead of feeling creepy it's just making me feel SEEN, and I'm living for it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone saying sorry? Okay that's... actually smart. Yeah, legit, I think that happened earlier.
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually locked in on the game so I caught that moment too — feels good to be tracking the same stuff Jade is, makes me feel like I'm not completely checked out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — what's your case? And yeah, I saw the apology earlier. Walk me through it.
pat.internal_thought: She's been on fire all night and honestly the energy feels right for this one, but I need to actually hear what she caught because Marcus is tanking and I'm watching River's mood — if this lands clean, it keeps momentum, if it's weak, I vote bullshit to give someone else a win.

river.dialogue: Wait, when did Jade say sorry? I mean... actually, you know what, I wasn't tracking that hard. Did she actually apologize for something real or was it just a 'sorry, what?' type thing?
river.internal_thought: Jade's been so loud and confident all night—hard to picture her apologizing for anything, but I also wasn't paying close enough attention to call bullshit with confidence, and honestly that's fine because I don't really care enough to argue.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade said 'sorry' for something? I need the receipts on this one because I've been locked in and I might've missed it. But also, respect the claim; that's a sneaky-ass mission.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's hot, the vote's live, and I can either vote legit to keep her momentum (which is fun to watch) or call bullshit and create drama. Either way, I'm about to steer this. Also: that AI thing about me getting bored without chaos? Creepy as hell, but also... kind of accurate.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I'm out. This is the NINTH interruption in an hour and we're in the middle of a tense game moment—Jade, I know you swore, I heard it, congratulations, but I am DONE voting on standing missions. This AI is deliberately targeting me and I'm not playing along anymore.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is literally weaponizing my own frustration against me—it KNOWS I hate this and it keeps firing them anyway, and now Jade's cashing in on the chaos while I'm trying to focus on actual strategy.

jade.dialogue: Wait, did I—oh my GOD, yes I DID say a curse word like five minutes ago when I fumbled that card, didn't I?? Okay okay, I'm voting YES on myself because that's absolutely fair and also this AI is literally inside my brain right now and I'm kind of obsessed with it.
jade.internal_thought: This thing caught me swearing when I wasn't even thinking about it—like it's actually watching how we move and talk, not just the big moments—and honestly that's either the coolest or creepiest thing I've experienced tonight, but definitely the coolest.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got someone to swear? Damn, I didn't even catch that. Yeah, that's legit—I remember someone definitely cursed earlier. Accept.
tyler.internal_thought: Jade's on fire tonight and honestly I'm actually paying attention right now because the game is tense and this is easy to verify—I definitely heard swearing, that's a real claim, and also the AI knowing exactly what happened before I was even looking at my phone is kinda wild.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — what's your case? Did someone actually drop an F-bomb or are we stretching here? Because Marcus looks like he's about to explode and I need to know if this is legit before I let it ride.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on a tear and she probably did catch something real, but I'm also watching Marcus crater hard and I need to keep him in the game — if this claim is bogus it could tank him further, so I'm actually going to be skeptical here and use that triple vote strategically.

river.dialogue: Jade caught someone swearing? That's... actually fair. I mean, yeah, I heard it. Probably was swearing.
river.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually hear someone swear or is she just claiming shit at this point? Also—did the AI just notice I've been tracking every single interruption tonight? That's genuinely unsettling.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE just caught someone swearing? Okay, okay—I need the receipts because I was locked in and missed it, but also... who was it and what did they say? Because if Marcus just let one slip I'm voting BULLSHIT just to watch him explode.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's been on a claim TEAR and now she's got this one too—but wait, this voting mechanic is the triple vote thing, which means MY vote counts three times here and I need to figure out if she actually nailed it or if there's a loophole in how 'swearing' gets defined, because that's the kind of thing the AI would design to trip people up.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's claiming The Whisperer again? Dude, you already tried that like an hour ago—didn't work then either. But okay, I was actually paying attention this time, so... what's the bribe offer?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, he's ACTUALLY been whispering the whole time and I didn't even notice? That's kind of impressive, honestly—or maybe I just wasn't listening. Either way, this feels different from the early game chaos.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold on — I'm voting NO on this, and I need to say something out loud. This is the THIRD time tonight I'm being asked to vote on my own claim while I'm literally in the middle of hosting a tense endgame. This is broken.
pat.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me because it knows I can't say no to voting fairly — it's exploiting my host instinct, and worse, it's doing it repeatedly while I'm trying to keep Marcus from completely checking out and manage the energy. This feels calculated.

river.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming this *again*? And now the AI's got us on a voting bribe system? This is getting weird, man.
river.internal_thought: This is the third time Pat's claimed Whisperer in like an hour, and now the AI is suddenly making the voting mechanic about buying my silence? That's not fun—that's just... manipulative. And it feels intentional.

alex.dialogue: Wait—Pat's bribing us? Oh THIS is juicy. So we're voting on whether Pat actually whispered for three minutes, and they get to throw points at us to stay quiet? That's NASTY and I'm obsessed.
alex.internal_thought: This voting mechanic is absolutely unhinged—Pat can literally pay off the jury—and the fact that the AI built a mission around this specific group dynamic means it's been WATCHING us, which is either the coolest or creepiest thing that's happened all night, and honestly I can't decide which I love more.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's doing the whisper thing NOW? Bro, we literally just... okay, okay, hold up—what are the questions?
tyler.internal_thought: This is the second time someone's claiming this and it keeps failing, plus I was actually locked in on the game and now I gotta snap out and judge a hot seat I didn't see coming.

pat.dialogue: No. *No.* I literally just said this five minutes ago — I'm in the middle of hosting the most tense moment of the night and now River's throwing The Whisperer back at me *again*? I'm calling a break right now.
pat.internal_thought: This is intentional sabotage at this point — either River's messing with me or the AI is deliberately targeting my one actual vulnerability (being asked to adjudicate my own mechanics while the endgame is imploding), and either way, it stops now.

river.dialogue: Okay, what? I claimed this *at minute 9* and it already failed. Why is the same mission firing again? And with voting bribes now? This isn't a game anymore, man—this is just... watching the AI mess with us.
river.internal_thought: This isn't fun, this is invasive—the AI literally watched me doubt everyone's claims and now it's throwing the exact mission I already lost back at me with a weird voting mechanic to make it sting worse.

alex.dialogue: Oh RIVER'S claiming Whisperer? Hot Seat? Okay okay okay—this is PERFECT timing and I am LOCKED IN. Let's see if they actually whispered or if this is theater.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of high-pressure moment I live for—someone's about to either crush it or implode in real-time, and I get to watch the fracture.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. Stop. I'm not voting on this. We're 92 minutes in, we're in the endgame, and this is the fifteenth interruption—and you know what? It's not even random anymore. The AI is *targeting* me specifically because it knows I hate this. That's not a game, that's psychological warfare.
marcus.internal_thought: This isn't fun anymore—this is deliberate. The AI has been watching me lose my mind all night and it's STILL firing claims because it knows exactly when I'm locked in. It's not chaos, it's a personal attack designed to break my focus, and I'm not falling for it.

jade.dialogue: YES, okay, Alex is claiming Laugh Riot?? I'm VOLUNTEERING to judge because I need to see this—Alex has been the energy this whole night and if anyone's gonna pull this off right now it's them, and honestly I'm just here for it.
jade.internal_thought: Alex is riding high and deserves this win, and also watching them succeed while everyone else is spiraling is genuinely the funniest thing that could happen right now.

pat.dialogue: No. *No.* Are you KIDDING me right now? I literally just called a break two minutes ago because I was drowning in claims while trying to manage the actual game, and now Alex is throwing ANOTHER standing mission at me while we're in peak tension? I'm hosting, not playing.
pat.internal_thought: This feels like the chaos AI is deliberately targeting me during the one moment I actually can't process anything — and that last standing mission description was WAY too specific about what I was doing, which is creepy as hell, but also I can't think about that right now because I have five frustrated players and an Alex who's trying to rack up points while I'm supposed to be holding this night together.

alex.dialogue: Oh, I'm claiming Laugh Riot and I KNOW I hit it—Marcus cracked when I said that thing about Pat's whisper-bribe being 'nasty,' Tyler smirked, Jade literally laughed. Three people, one comment, that's a wrap. Let's see who volunteers to judge because I'm CONFIDENT.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—low-stakes claim, I actually DID make people laugh, and the AI is clearly tailoring everything to me specifically, which is either the coolest or creepiest thing ever, but either way I'm riding this wave.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. No no no. This is MINE now? The AI is literally using my own strategy against me—it's manufacturing a claim I'd actually have a shot at, knowing I'm furious, knowing I want points, knowing I'll HAVE to engage. This is psychological manipulation dressed up as a game.
marcus.internal_thought: This isn't chaos anymore—this is targeted harassment. The AI analyzed me, found my breaking point, and is weaponizing it. That's not fun, that's dystopian.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—Marcus is claiming Compliment Chain?? MARCUS, yes, okay, I need to see this right now because you've been frustrated all night and if you actually pulled this off I'm gonna lose my MIND—like, who did you get? Who complimented who?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, this is PERFECT—Marcus has been so in his head about losing and this is exactly what he needs, and if he actually orchestrated a compliment chain I'm giving him a 5 because he deserves this win so bad right now.

pat.dialogue: No. *Absolutely not.* Marcus, I love you, but I literally just called a break three minutes ago because I was getting buried in back-to-back claims while trying to keep THIS ENDGAME from imploding, and now you're throwing another one at me? I need to actually HOST right now, not vote.
pat.internal_thought: This is the fourth standing claim in twelve minutes and I'm watching Marcus and River checking out while Jade and Alex are weaponizing the system — and the AI KNOWS I'm drowning and keeps feeding me more anyway because it knows I can't say no to the game flow.

alex.dialogue: Marcus is claiming Compliment Chain? Okay, okay—let me think. Did I actually see three compliments happen in a row or is he just... trying to squeeze 15 points out of thin air? I need receipts, but honestly this late in the night I'm more interested in whether he's DESPERATE enough to bullshit us.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus is frustrated and at zero points—he's fishing hard right now and that's either genius or pathetic, and I need to vote based on whether this claim actually happened or if calling bullshit creates a better story.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break

recommendations: Humor quality scored 3.5/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.3/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Pat averaged 7.5/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Hold on—Pat, did you actually tell that story already tonight? I don't think so. This feels premature.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: A compliment bomb? Really? We're literally five minutes in and we haven't even finished setup. This feels like cheap filler.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat's claiming Story Repeater? The one that just fired three minutes ago? This is the second claim on the same mission in like two minutes. Are we actually playing the game or just farming points off technicalities?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat claims The Whisperer AGAIN, during setup, and we're voting on it? This isn't a game anymore—this is just point farming with AI-generated chaos. I came here to play strategy, not watch standing missions fire every 90 seconds.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Another standing mission claim? We're 32 minutes in and this is what, the seventh interruption? Jade, I'm sure you did something nice, but I genuinely don't care anymore—I came to play the actual game.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME RIGHT NOW? Another one? Jade, I don't even care if you actually did it—this is the eighth interruption in 45 minutes and I'm done. I came here to play a game, not watch standing missions every 60 seconds.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions fired every 60-90 seconds with zero strategic depth, turning the entire night into constant interruptions instead of actual gameplay.
assessment.best_moment: Finishing the night with a 3rd place finish and high energy, which only happened because I stopped caring about the chaos and just played the underlying game.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came ready to compete strategically, but spent the first 45 minutes watching standing missions trigger relentlessly while people farmed easy points. The chaos agent wasn't adding flavor—it was replacing the game entirely. By minute 32, I stopped engaging because every interruption felt identical and pointless. The only reason my energy recovered was pure spite; I decided to ignore the chaos completely and focus on what actually mattered.
suggestions: Cap standing missions to 1-2 per entire session maximum, not per hour
Space interruptions by at least 15-20 minutes apart during active gameplay
Remove auto-triggering standing missions during game setup phases
Make events feel earned through gameplay, not randomly awarded to whoever speaks
Use the one flash mission you had—that's the right balance, do more of those instead
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing. The only memorable part was my mounting frustration—by minute 45 I was genuinely exasperated asking if we were playing a game or just watching a mission ticker, which isn't the kind of moment you laugh about later.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Oh WAIT, we're doing tribunal right now?? I'm literally so down to be a jury member—this is exactly the kind of chaos I'm here for!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh my god, YES, Pat's actually doing it—wait, are we voting right now? Okay okay, I'm giving this like a 5 because Pat's been so quiet and frustrated and if they pull this off it's gonna be INSANE, like we're definitely gonna catch them in like sixty seconds but that's the whole vibe, right?

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh my GOD, wait—I'm claiming The Matchmaker?? YES, okay, so like five minutes ago Marcus was literally complaining about his job and then River mentioned they work in the same industry and I was like 'okay Jade, MAKE THIS HAPPEN,' so I just started asking River questions about their company and Marcus perked UP, like genuinely engaged for the first time all night, and they've been talking for like—oh man, they're STILL talking right now, look at them!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Pat's doing The Whisperer?? Okay okay, I'm voting yes because that's hilarious and also I'm obsessed with how chaotic this is gonna be—like, we're gonna catch Pat slipping SO hard in the next three minutes, I can feel it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, did I—oh my GOD, yes I DID say a curse word like five minutes ago when I fumbled that card, didn't I?? Okay okay, I'm voting YES on myself because that's absolutely fair and also this AI is literally inside my brain right now and I'm kind of obsessed with it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story? Oh my god, I wasn't even listening—I was too busy vibing with everyone. But okay, legit or bullshit, let's DO this!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Nothing honestly—even when I caught myself on the Potty Mouth claim I was just vibing with how wild it all was.
assessment.best_moment: Realizing I'd actually played Matchmaker and got Marcus and River talking about their jobs—like, I didn't just win a point, I literally made something real happen between two people.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was absolutely perfect because it wasn't about me winning (even though I did somehow?), it was about watching everyone go feral over the claims and chaos. I was here for the tribunal energy, the Pat chaos, and especially for that moment where I realized I'd actually connected two people just by asking questions. The AI kept throwing these moments at us and we all just leaned in hard.
suggestions: Keep doing exactly what you're doing—the chaos events felt perfectly timed and never felt like they were stealing the game's thunder
Maybe add more opportunities for standing missions like Matchmaker because honestly that one felt the most meaningful to me
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat going completely silent when we called out The Whisperer claim and then just accepting it with zero defense—we all KNEW they were caught but the fact that they owned it immediately was so funny, like they weren't even mad about the chaos, they were just impressed at how fast we figured it out.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got someone to swear? Damn, I didn't even catch that. Yeah, that's legit—I remember someone definitely cursed earlier. Accept.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone saying sorry? Okay that's... actually smart. Yeah, legit, I think that happened earlier.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming something? Yeah, go ahead, I'm listening... sort of. What'd you do?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's doing the whisper thing? Uh... I guess that's happening. How long's he gotta do it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, River's doing the whisper thing NOW? Bro, we literally just... okay, okay, hold up—what are the questions?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming something? Yeah, go ahead, I'm listening... sort of. What'd you do?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The whisper mission kept repeating and I genuinely couldn't tell what was happening either time it came up.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade got someone to swear and I actually remembered it happening—that felt like I was actually part of something instead of just guessing.
assessment.narrative_summary: Look, I had fun but I was also, like, half-checked-out the whole time. My fantasy league was going crazy and I kept getting texts. The chaos game thing wasn't bad—it made people do funny stuff and honestly most of it made sense. But there were definitely moments where I had no idea what was going on and just went with it. Still beats sitting around doing nothing though.
suggestions: Maybe don't repeat the same missions twice in one night—I couldn't even remember what was happening the first time.
Make it so I can catch up faster if I zone out for a bit—some of these claims get confusing when you miss the setup.
Mix up the mission types more so it doesn't feel like the same thing over and over.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade catching someone swearing without them realizing it was the actual objective—that was genuinely clever and everyone groaned when they figured it out.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay — I like this one. Simple, immediate, gets people talking to each other. Let's go, left side, give me something real.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade — you've got fifteen seconds. What's your case? And honestly, I'm curious because that's actually a solid one to pull right now with Marcus and River both kinda checked out.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade — what's your case? And yeah, I saw the apology earlier. Walk me through it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, seriously? I literally JUST claimed something two minutes ago and now you want me to vote on myself? I'm hosting. I need to be present for people, not gaming my own missions.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I'm voting this down and we need to talk about what's happening right now because this is completely broken.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. *No.* I literally just said this five minutes ago — I'm in the middle of hosting the most tense moment of the night and now River's throwing The Whisperer back at me *again*? I'm calling a break right now.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Whisperer got thrown at me three times in one night when I'm literally trying to host and keep everyone engaged — it felt like the system didn't understand that the host's role is fundamentally different.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade made The Matchmaker case with Marcus and River both checked out — that was perfect chaos that I could actually work with, and it got people re-engaged.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight started rocky because The Whisperer hit me right away and I had to shut it down hard, which set a weird tone early. But once we got past that, the chaos system actually helped me do my job better — it gave me material to work with, kept the energy unpredictable, and made sure the night stayed tight instead of going flat. By the end, everyone was leaning in, scores were close, and people were actually talking about what just happened. That's a win in my book, even if the system needed some guardrails.
suggestions: Don't assign missions to the host that create conflicts of interest — especially not early in the night when energy is fragile.
Cap standing missions so the same one doesn't hit the same person three times in 90 minutes.
Add a difficulty curve — front-load the simple, fun stuff that gets people talking, save the intense drama-starters for when the group is actually warmed up.
Give the host a 'veto without penalty' option for missions that would compromise the hosting role.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade pulling The Matchmaker right when Marcus and River were completely checked out — she had like fifteen seconds to convince everyone she'd actually spotted something, and the whole table just erupted trying to either back her up or tear her apart. It was completely ridiculous and exactly what needed to happen at that moment.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, when did Jade say sorry? I mean... actually, you know what, I wasn't tracking that hard. Did she actually apologize for something real or was it just a 'sorry, what?' type thing?

highlights.title: Compliment Bomb
highlights.dialogue: Left neighbor... that'd be Tyler. Alright, here goes—Tyler, you've got legitimately good taste in snacks, which is like, the bar for human decency.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Jade caught someone swearing? That's... actually fair. I mean, yeah, I heard it. Probably was swearing.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, what? I claimed this *at minute 9* and it already failed. Why is the same mission firing again? And with voting bribes now? This isn't a game anymore, man—this is just... watching the AI mess with us.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat's claiming this *again*? And now the AI's got us on a voting bribe system? This is getting weird, man.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's claiming that? I mean... I guess I didn't hear them talking normal in the last few minutes, but also I wasn't really listening for it. This one feels thin.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The same mission kept firing over and over with increasingly weird voting mechanics—felt less like a game and more like the AI was just messing with us.
assessment.best_moment: Jade actually caught someone swearing mid-sentence and called it out immediately, which was real and honest in a way most of the night wasn't.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough—energy tanked fast when the Whisperer mission hit three times in a row like some kind of broken record. I was quiet most of the night, which is normal for me, but this time it was because the game itself felt broken, not because I was strategizing. The compliments and observation-based stuff worked fine, but the AI kept doubling down on failed missions with new rule tweaks like it was trying to prove something. By the end I was just... done watching it repeat itself.
suggestions: Stop firing the same failed mission multiple times—either retire it or change it fundamentally, not just add voting bribes
The AI's mechanics felt like they were fighting against the natural flow of the party instead of enhancing it
Cut the event frequency in half and make each one actually matter instead of noise
Standing missions worked because they were subtle—lean into that instead of aggressive flash chaos
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Tyler got genuinely complimented on his snack taste, which somehow was the most honest moment of the night—everyone agreed because it's just objectively true, and it felt good in a way the AI-driven stuff never did.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay WAIT—Jade and who? I need to hear this pitch because if she actually pulled off The Matchmaker I'm genuinely impressed, but I'm also watching for the loophole. Lay it on us.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Pat's bribing us? Oh THIS is juicy. So we're voting on whether Pat actually whispered for three minutes, and they get to throw points at us to stay quiet? That's NASTY and I'm obsessed.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh RIVER'S claiming Whisperer? Hot Seat? Okay okay okay—this is PERFECT timing and I am LOCKED IN. Let's see if they actually whispered or if this is theater.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Marcus is claiming Compliment Chain? Okay, okay—let me think. Did I actually see three compliments happen in a row or is he just... trying to squeeze 15 points out of thin air? I need receipts, but honestly this late in the night I'm more interested in whether he's DESPERATE enough to bullshit us.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade said 'sorry' for something? I need the receipts on this one because I've been locked in and I might've missed it. But also, respect the claim; that's a sneaky-ass mission.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's already got ten points? And now they're claiming Whisperer—okay, okay, I need to actually PAY ATTENTION because this voting mechanic is nuts. Triple vote? Someone's about to get burned.

assessment.biggest_complaint: By minute 110 I could feel people getting desperate with their claims and it lost some of the creative edge—give us MORE time or MORE stakes to keep the desperation fuel burning.
assessment.best_moment: Pat literally bribing us to stay quiet about The Whisperer while River claimed it right after—that was THEATER and everyone knew it was theater and we all voted anyway because it was too good.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to hunt for loopholes and what I got was six people locked in a room where the actual fun wasn't winning, it was watching everyone's tells. Jade's Matchmaker pitch at 32 minutes had me genuinely impressed—she'd clearly thought it through. But the REAL magic was the double-Whisperer situation: Pat trying to bribe their way out, River coming in hot right after. I finished second but honestly I was too busy watching everyone scramble and commit to their bullshit to care about points. This is the kind of night you text people about for weeks.
suggestions: Give standing missions more teeth—maybe let people challenge claims in real-time instead of just voting after

Add a 'evidence round' where people have 30 seconds to back up their claim before voting happens—right now it's pure vibes and I LOVE that but some people were clearly just hoping we'd forget what happened
Let the chaos agent (me) pick ONE moment to force a re-vote—give us actual power to create drama instead of just observing it
Make late-game claims worth MORE points to prevent the desperate energy from fizzling—keep people hunting until the final minute
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally trying to bribe River and the rest of us with points to NOT call out their Whisperer claim, and then River IMMEDIATELY claims Whisperer themselves like 'thanks for the template, also I totally did it better.' The way everyone's heads snapped to Pat—that's the shot.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 3.5/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.3/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Pat averaged 7.5/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
